title: Floating soy rose candles
subtitle: Small disc-shaped soy wax candles scented with rose that float in a bowl of water
excerpt: Floating candles are poured into shallow disc moulds and scented with rose essential oil. Made in soy wax at a slightly higher melt point than container soy, they float level and burn for 3 to 4 hours each. Grouped in a wide bowl of water, they make a clean table centrepiece.
sourceNotes: Soy wax for floating candles (slightly higher melt point pillar-blend soy): Lone Star Candle Supply technical reference (2022). Fragrance load at 6% for soy pillar/floater blend: Bramble Berry candle guide. Rose essential oil safety in candles: IFRA category 12 standard (2021). Burn time for 5 cm floater disc at 2 cm depth: approximately 3 to 4 hours based on industry test data.

recipe.yieldDescription: 6 floating candles (each 5 cm diameter, 2 cm deep)
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily. Pour all moulds from the same batch for consistent appearance.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Floating candles store well for months and can be made well in advance.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Floating candles keep three years in a cool place. The rose fragrance fades after around two years. Discard if the wax discolours or the fragrance has completely gone.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which wax is poured into a mould. Floating candle soy blend pours at 65 to 70 degrees C for a smooth, level top.

content.text: Floating candles are simple to make and store well until needed. This recipe uses a slightly higher melt point soy wax (sometimes labelled pillar-blend or floater soy) rather than the softer container grades, which gives the disc enough firmness to float level without bending. The rose essential oil is added just before pouring. A set of six floaters in a wide bowl or trough looks best with unscented water, letting the candle fragrance carry.

items.prepNote: higher melt point soy, suitable for pillars and floating candles

content.text: Melt wax over a water bath only. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a kitchen fire blanket within reach.

Press the pre-tabbed wicks into the centre of each mould cavity, or hold them in place with a small piece of putty at the base. The wick tab sits on the bottom of the mould; this becomes the top of the finished candle (the candle is inverted when unmoulded). If using silicone moulds, a light application of mould release is optional.
Melt the soy pillar wax to 75 degrees C over a water bath. Cool to 65 to 70 degrees C and stir in the rose essential oil for 2 minutes. Pour into the moulds at the
of 65 to 70 degrees C. Overfilling causes irregular edges; fill to 5 mm from the rim.

Leave the moulds undisturbed at room temperature for 2 hours or until fully set. For silicone moulds, flex the sides gently to release the candles. The flat top (the mould bottom) of each disc becomes the base that goes into the water; the smooth bottom of the mould becomes the top of the floating candle.

Place floating candles in a wide, low bowl or trough of water deep enough so they float freely (at least 4 cm). Space them so the flames don't overlap. Trim wicks to 5 mm before each use. Never leave burning candles unattended. Each candle burns for 3 to 4 hours.
Store in a cool place away from direct light. Stacking is fine if separated by a sheet of greaseproof paper. The candles keep three years. The rose fragrance is most noticeable in the first year.
